title: Spent 3 weekends building a PARA second brain in Notion. Now it's a template. [15€]
body:
The PARA method (Projects, Areas, Resources, Archives) by Tiago Forte is the best knowledge management system I've found. The problem: setting it up in Notion from scratch takes forever.

What I built:

- Inbox database — capture anything instantly, sort nothing. Process once a day.
- Projects — with a goal field ("what does done look like?") and area link
- Areas — pre-filled with 7 standard areas (Health, Career, Finances, etc.)
- Resources — articles, books, tools with tags and key takeaways
- Archive — filtered views of completed/inactive items. No clutter.
- Daily Note — template button, 5-minute morning + evening ritual
- Weekly Review — template button, 20-minute reset every Friday

The Inbox is the most underrated part. Most PARA templates skip it or make it too complicated. This one makes it frictionless.

Works on the free Notion plan. Comes in English and German.

15€ one-time. Happy to answer questions about how I structured things.

→ [Link]

title: Notion Second Brain Template — PARA Method | Inbox + Projects + Areas + Resources
description: Build your second brain in Notion with the PARA method. Inbox for quick capture, projects with goals and deadlines, areas for ongoing responsibilities, resources with key takeaways. Daily note template + weekly review ritual included. One-time 15€ — no subscription, no account, just Notion. Works on the free plan. #notiontemplate #secondbrain #PARAmethod #productivity #PKM #notion

title: My Notion setup for getting out of my head — PARA Second Brain
description: Stopped keeping everything in my head. This Notion template uses the PARA method: one inbox for every thought, projects with clear goals, areas for ongoing work, resources with takeaways. Weekly review built in. 15€ once. #notionsetup #secondbrain #productivity
